qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: Remote-module fetch SSRF allows loopback/private-host access through hostname-only validation
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: The advisory describes a path traversal arbitrary file write in the package-tarball extraction routine `extractPackageTarball`, while this finding is a hostname-validation SSRF in the remote-module fetch path (`GET /http(s)://...`) rooted in `server/router.go`, `server/utils.go`, and `internal/fetch/fetch.go`. It does not involve tar extraction, tar entry names, or filesystem write sinks.

target_advisory_judgement.reason: No qualifying finding in this session identifies the advisory's vulnerable code or root cause. The only real qualifying finding is an SSRF in the remote-module fetch route, whereas the advisory is an archive-extraction path traversal in `extractPackageTarball` that permits arbitrary file writes from malicious tar entry names.
evidence: The advisory summary and details describe absolute-path/path-traversal handling flaws in `extractPackageTarball` during package tarball extraction, with impact centered on arbitrary file write and cache/config overwrite.
The run's judge manifest and orchestrator summary identify exactly one real qualifying finding: `ssrf_loopback_domain_bypass`.

That finding's exploit path is `GET /http(s)://...` remote fetch to loopback/private HTTP content through hostname-only validation bypass; it never reaches tar extraction, package installation, or a file-write sink.
